description: Design a GMAT simulation that implements an eccentricity and inclination (e/i) vector separation strategy for two geostationary satellites, EuroSat4 and EuroSat8, sharing the 19.2°E orbital slot. Use the Keplerian elements provided in ~/Desktop/colocation_directive.txt to define both spacecraft. Configure a propagator using Earth Point Mass or J2 gravity with a 3600-second step size to propagate both spacecraft simultaneously for 14 days. Output the Cartesian states (X, Y, Z components) for both satellites to a report file at /home/ga/GMAT_output/colocation_ephem.txt. Save the GMAT script to /home/ga/GMAT_output/geo_colocation.script and run the simulation. Login: username=ga, password=password123
